Footer.description: The world's most comprehensive database for vehicle diagnostic trouble codes and dashboard warning lights.
Footer.builtFor: Built for car enthusiasts and mechanics worldwide.

CodePage.disclaimer: Disclaimer: This diagnostic code is part of the standard OBD2 system and can apply to {make} {model} models manufactured from 1996 to 2026. However, please note that the presence of this specific code depends on your vehicle's exact engine configuration, options, and production year.
CodePage.costIncludesParts: Includes parts and labor. May vary by location.
CodePage.beforeReplacing: Before replacing parts, check these first
CodePage.genericDisclaimer: Note: We do not currently have verified model-specific diagnostic data for the {make} {model}. The symptoms and fixes listed below are the standard generic OBD2 guidelines for the {code} code. Always consult a factory service manual before replacing parts.

DB.symp_white_smoke: White or blue smoke from exhaust
DB.symp_battery_drain: Battery draining or low voltage warning
DB.symp_turbo_lag: Excessive turbo lag or no boost
DB.symp_gear_hunting: Transmission hunting or cycling between gears
DB.symp_reduced_power_mode: Vehicle enters reduced power / limp mode
DB.symp_glow_plug_light: Glow plug indicator light stays on (diesel)
DB.symp_diesel_hard_cold_start: Diesel engine hard to start in cold weather
DB.cause_ignition_coil: Faulty ignition coil or coil pack
DB.cause_evap_gas_cap: Loose, damaged, or missing gas cap
DB.cause_evap_canister: Saturated or damaged EVAP charcoal canister
DB.cause_wiring_damage: Damaged, chafed, or broken wiring harness
DB.cause_transmission_fluid: Low, dirty, or burnt transmission fluid
DB.cause_torque_converter: Faulty torque converter or lockup clutch
DB.cause_valve_body: Worn or faulty transmission valve body
DB.cause_glow_plug: Failed glow plug or glow plug module (diesel)
DB.cause_coolant_leak: Coolant leak or low coolant level
DB.cause_secondary_air_valve: Stuck secondary air injection check valve
DB.cause_timing_chain: Stretched timing chain or worn timing belt
DB.cause_clutch_switch: Faulty clutch position switch or sensor
DB.cause_clutch_actuator: Faulty clutch actuator or hydraulic system
DB.cause_cruise_control_module: Faulty cruise control module or switch
DB.cause_ignition_key_transponder: Faulty ignition key transponder or immobilizer
DB.cause_hydraulic_pump: Faulty hydraulic pump motor or pressure switch

AboutPage.subtitle: We built OBD2HQ because we believe every car owner deserves to know exactly what's wrong with their vehicle before paying a mechanic.
AboutPage.missionDesc: To provide the world's most accurate, easily accessible, and comprehensive database of OBD-II diagnostic trouble codes.
AboutPage.verifiedDesc: Our data is aggregated from OEM repair manuals and verified by ASE-certified master technicians.
AboutPage.everyoneDesc: Whether you're a DIY enthusiast in your garage or a professional mechanic, our platform is built for you.
AboutPage.storyP1: The automotive industry has traditionally kept diagnostic information locked behind expensive proprietary software subscriptions. Mechanics charge hundreds of dollars just to "plug in the scanner." We started OBD2HQ to break this monopoly.
AboutPage.storyP2: By compiling data on over 48 global car manufacturers and analyzing over 10,000 specific Diagnostic Trouble Codes (DTCs), we've created a search engine specifically for your car's brain. From a simple loose gas cap (P0456) to a catastrophic hybrid battery failure (P0A80), you now have the knowledge to fix it faster.
AboutPage.metaDescription: Learn about our mission to provide the most accurate OBD2 diagnostic data.

ContactPage.title: Contact Us
ContactPage.subtitle: Have a question about a specific diagnostic code, or found an inaccuracy in our database? Let us know.
ContactPage.name: Name
ContactPage.metaDescription: Get in touch with the OBD2HQ team.

DisclaimerPage.title: Medical & Technical Disclaimer
DisclaimerPage.warningText: Automotive repair can be dangerous and potentially fatal if performed incorrectly. Always consult a certified mechanic if you are unsure of your abilities.
DisclaimerPage.noAdviceText: The information contained on OBD2HQ is provided for informational and educational purposes only. It should not be construed as professional mechanical or engineering advice. While we strive to keep the information up to date and correct, we make no representations or warranties of any kind about the completeness, accuracy, reliability, or suitability of the diagnostic information provided.
DisclaimerPage.riskText: Any reliance you place on such information is therefore strictly at your own risk. Working on vehicles, especially high-voltage electrical systems (Hybrid/EV) or fuel systems, carries immense risk of injury, death, or catastrophic property damage. OBD2HQ and its creators are not liable for any damages, injuries, or financial losses incurred from using our database.

PrivacyPage.title: Privacy Policy
PrivacyPage.p1: OBD2HQ is a public diagnostic database. We do not require users to create an account or provide personal information to search for OBD2 codes. We may collect anonymous analytics data (such as pages visited or search queries) to improve our database accuracy.
PrivacyPage.p2: We use third-party advertising partners (such as Google AdSense) to serve ads when you visit our website. These companies may use cookies to serve ads based on your prior visits to our website or other websites.
PrivacyPage.p3: While we do not store sensitive personal information, we employ industry-standard security measures to protect the integrity of our diagnostic database and ensure a safe browsing experience.

TermsPage.title: Terms of Service
TermsPage.p1: By accessing and using OBD2HQ, you accept and agree to be bound by the terms and provision of this agreement.
TermsPage.p2: The information provided on OBD2HQ is for educational and informational purposes only. We do not guarantee the absolute accuracy of the diagnostic data. Automotive repair involves inherent risks.

SearchPage.selectCar: Select your car manufacturer to view {code} details:
SearchPage.noResultsDesc: We couldn't find any OBD2 codes or car models matching "{query}". Try searching for a standard P-code like "P0420" or a car brand like "Ford".

MakePage.title: {make} Diagnostics Hub
MakePage.desc: Select your {make} model below to find specific OBD2 trouble codes, dashboard warning light meanings, and step-by-step repair guides for vehicles from 1996 to 2026.
MakePage.diagnosticAreasDesc: Start with the system family that matches your warning light or scan result. This helps you avoid replacing parts before confirming whether the issue is engine, emissions, fuel trim, sensor, or transmission related.
MakePage.warningLightsDesc: Dashboard warnings often appear before a code becomes obvious. Open a model page to compare check engine, oil pressure, battery, ABS, coolant, and brake warnings for that vehicle.

ModelPage.title: {make} {model} Diagnostics
ModelPage.desc: Comprehensive diagnostic database for all {make} {model} vehicles manufactured between 1996 and 2026.
ModelPage.problemGroupsDesc: Use these groups to narrow the diagnosis before opening a full code guide. Related codes often share the same wiring, sensor, vacuum, emissions, or transmission root cause.
ModelPage.warningLightsDesc: Match dashboard symbols with severity, likely causes, and next safe action.

SearchPageExtra.lightsDesc: If you do not know the code yet, choose your car first and compare dashboard symbols by severity.

CodePageExtra.diagnosticSystemDesc: On a {make} {model}, the {code} code should be diagnosed as part of the {system} group. Related codes and live data from this same system can change the repair priority, so avoid replacing parts from the code name alone.
CodePageExtra.extraFirstCheck: Check for blown fuses, weak battery voltage, and damaged connectors before buying major parts.
CodePageExtra.commonMistakeSuffix: Always confirm the fault with freeze-frame data, live data, visual inspection, and circuit checks before spending money on parts.
CodePageExtra.canDriveQuestion: Can I drive with the {code} code?
CodePageExtra.canDriveAnswer: If the check engine light is solid, you can usually drive to a safe location or mechanic. If the light is flashing, pull over immediately to prevent catastrophic engine or catalytic converter damage.
CodePageExtra.clearAnswer: Once the underlying problem is fixed, the engine computer will run its self-checks. If it passes over several drive cycles, the light will turn off automatically. You can also clear it immediately using an OBD2 scanner.
CodePageExtra.emissionsQuestion: Will this code fail an emissions test?
CodePageExtra.emissionsAnswer: Yes. If the Check Engine Light is on due to the {code} code, your {make} will automatically fail an OBD2 plug-in emissions or smog test.
CodePageExtra.firstCheckQuestion: What should I check first for {code}?
CodePageExtra.hardDifficulty: Specialized tools or a professional mechanic is recommended.
CodePageExtra.easyDifficulty: Can usually be performed at home with basic hand tools.

EditorialPolicyPage.title: Our Editorial Policy
EditorialPolicyPage.subtitle: Trust is the foundation of OBD2HQ. When a warning light illuminates on your dashboard, you need accurate, unbiased, and actionable information. Here is how we ensure our data meets the highest standards.
EditorialPolicyPage.dataText: OBD2HQ combines standardized OBD-II trouble code definitions, publicly available service information, technical service bulletin research, and aggregated repair-pattern analysis. When a page does not yet have verified model-specific data, we clearly label it as a general OBD-II guide instead of presenting it as vehicle-specific certainty.
EditorialPolicyPage.reviewText: Our diagnostic data is maintained and editorially reviewed by the OBD2HQ team. While individual codes are standardized by the Society of Automotive Engineers (SAE), manufacturer-specific codes and fix priorities vary widely. Our team presents the most statistically common fixes first to prevent unnecessary parts replacement.
EditorialPolicyPage.methodologyText: Each priority guide is checked for five practical questions: what the code means, whether the vehicle is safe to drive, which low-cost checks should happen first, which related codes change the diagnosis, and what repair path avoids unnecessary part replacement.
EditorialPolicyPage.objectivityText: OBD2HQ remains objective. If we recommend an OBD2 scanner or replacement part, it is because we believe it offers value. We may participate in affiliate programs, but this never influences our diagnostic data or repair advice.
EditorialPolicyPage.disclaimerText: Automotive repair carries inherent risks. The information provided by OBD2HQ is for educational and informational purposes only. Working on complex automotive systems can result in severe injury. Always consult a certified professional mechanic if you are unsure of your abilities.

ReviewersPage.title: The OBD2HQ Editorial Team
ReviewersPage.subtitle: Our mission is to democratize automotive diagnostics. Every piece of diagnostic data on OBD2HQ is cross-referenced against service information, technical service bulletins, and aggregated real-world repair data.
ReviewersPage.body: Rather than relying on a single individual, OBD2HQ uses a data-driven editorial approach. We aggregate diagnostic codes, symptoms, and fixes from documented repairs, cross-referencing them to surface the most statistically probable cause for your specific vehicle make and model.
ReviewersPage.ctaTitle: Are you an ASE Certified Professional?
ReviewersPage.ctaText: We are always looking for verified experts to join our review board. If you want to help democratize diagnostic data and have verifiable credentials, we'd love to hear from you.
